title: The Country Channel – Radio Station Website with Admin Dashboard

requirements.text: Build a public-facing homepage for 'The Country Channel' radio station that displays the station name/logo, a hero section with a live-stream play button (non-functional placeholder), a featured shows section, and a DJ roster section.

quotes: create a radio station website called the country channel

acceptanceCriteria: Homepage renders 'The Country Channel' branding prominently
Hero section includes a play button styled as a live-stream trigger
Featured Shows section displays show cards fetched from the backend
DJ Roster section displays DJ cards fetched from the backend
Page is fully responsive on mobile and desktop

requirements.text: Create a Shows page that lists all radio shows with their name, description, schedule/time slot, and associated DJ name.
acceptanceCriteria: Shows page lists all shows retrieved from the backend
Each show card displays name, description, schedule, and DJ name
Page is accessible from the site navigation

requirements.text: Create a DJs page that lists all DJs with their name, bio, photo URL (or placeholder), and associated shows.
acceptanceCriteria: DJs page lists all DJs retrieved from the backend
Each DJ card shows name, bio, photo/avatar, and linked shows
Page is accessible from the site navigation

requirements.text: Implement a backend data model and CRUD API for Shows. Each show must store: id, title, description, scheduleDay (e.g. Monday), scheduleTime, djId (optional reference), and a createdAt timestamp.
acceptanceCriteria: createShow, getShows, getShow, updateShow, deleteShow functions exist in the actor
Shows are persisted in stable storage
Each show has all required fields: id, title, description, scheduleDay, scheduleTime, djId, createdAt

requirements.text: Implement a backend data model and CRUD API for DJs. Each DJ must store: id, name, bio, photoUrl, and a createdAt timestamp.
acceptanceCriteria: createDJ, getDJs, getDJ, updateDJ, deleteDJ functions exist in the actor
DJs are persisted in stable storage
Each DJ has all required fields: id, name, bio, photoUrl, createdAt

requirements.text: Build an Admin Dashboard accessible via a protected route (/admin). The dashboard must have a sidebar with navigation to 'Manage Shows' and 'Manage DJs' sections, plus a summary overview (total shows count, total DJs count).

quotes: admin dashboard to manage shows and djs

acceptanceCriteria: Admin dashboard is accessible at /admin
Sidebar includes links to Manage Shows and Manage DJs
Overview panel displays total count of shows and DJs
Layout is distinct from the public-facing site

requirements.text: Implement a Shows management interface in the admin dashboard allowing the admin to create, edit, and delete shows via a form modal. The form must include fields for title, description, scheduleDay, scheduleTime, and DJ selection (dropdown populated from the DJs list).
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can create a new show via a form modal
Admin can edit an existing show's details
Admin can delete a show with a confirmation prompt
DJ selection dropdown is populated from the backend DJ list
Changes are immediately reflected in the shows list without full page reload

requirements.text: Implement a DJs management interface in the admin dashboard allowing the admin to create, edit, and delete DJs via a form modal. The form must include fields for name, bio, and photoUrl.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can create a new DJ via a form modal
Admin can edit an existing DJ's details
Admin can delete a DJ with a confirmation prompt
Changes are immediately reflected in the DJs list without full page reload

requirements.text: Apply a cohesive visual theme throughout the site inspired by classic country music aesthetics: warm earthy tones (browns, tans, burnt oranges), bold serif/slab typography for headings, woodgrain or rustic texture accents, and a dark rich background for the admin dashboard. Enforce this theme consistently across all public and admin pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Color palette uses warm earthy/rustic tones consistently across all pages
Headings use bold serif or slab-serif style typography
Admin dashboard uses a darker variant of the same theme
No default browser or generic styling is visible

constraints: All backend logic must reside in a single Motoko actor (backend/main.mo)
Do not use external authentication; admin route can use a simple local guard for now
Do not integrate real audio streaming — the play button is a non-functional placeholder
nonGoals: Real-time audio streaming or podcast hosting
Internet Identity authentication for the admin

required: Rustic country radio station logo with a vintage microphone and guitar silhouette, warm earthy tones on a dark background (country-channel-logo.dim_400x200.png)
Wide hero banner with a moody twilight sky over rolling farmland and a broadcast tower, warm amber and deep brown tones (hero-banner.dim_1440x600.png)
Decorative horizontal divider with a rope or woodgrain texture strip (divider-texture.dim_1440x40.png)
